content.problemStatement:
You are given two strings PPP and QQQ. Consider some uniformly randomly and independently chosen non-empty prefix XXX and non-empty suffix YYY of PPP. A string ZZZ is formed by concatenating XXX and YYY in this order. Find the probability that ZZZ is a substring of QQQ.

It can be shown that the answer can be represented as a fraction U/VU/VU/V, where UUU and VVV are non-negative integers and VVV is coprime with 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353. You should compute UŌŗģVŌłÆ1U \cdot V^{-1}UŌŗģVŌłÆ1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353, where VŌłÆ1V^{-1}VŌłÆ1 denotes the multiplicative inverse of VVV modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.
constraints: PPP and QQQ contain only lowercase English letters
content.inputFormat:
The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
The first line of each test case contains a single string PPP.
The second line contains a single string QQQ.
content.outputFormat: For each test case, print a single line containing one integer UŌŗģVŌłÆ1U \cdot V^{-1}UŌŗģVŌłÆ1 modulo 998,244,353998,244,353998,244,353.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Example case 1: There are 444 possible prefixes XXX and 444 suffixes YYY. The only combinations that result in a string ZZZ that is a substring of QQQ are:

XXX = "a", YYY = "ac"
XXX = "a", YYY = "c"
XXX = "ab", YYY = "c"

The probability that ZZZ is a substring of QQQ is 3/163/163/16.
